channel.title: Research on Dingyi Zhao
channel.description: Recent content in Research on Dingyi Zhao

item.title: Inferring Resolved Kinematics from Multi-Band Imaging
item.description:
This direction develops computer-vision models that map imaging data to resolved physical fields.

Focus

  • Generative modeling for kinematic reconstruction.
  • End-to-end training with simulation-informed supervision.
  • Evaluation of physical consistency and uncertainty.

Outcome

The method aims to make kinematic information accessible at scale for surveys without IFU-level spectroscopy.

item.title: Halo Mass as a Driver of Central Galaxy Quenching
item.description:
This project investigates how halo-scale processes regulate star formation shutdown in central galaxies.

Focus

  • Compare halo mass against stellar mass and structural indicators.
  • Build controlled samples to separate confounding effects.
  • Quantify quenching trends across mass and environment bins.

Outcome

Results support the interpretation that halo mass plays a dominant role in central-galaxy quenching.

item.title: Machine Learning for Halo Mass Estimation
item.description:
This line of work focuses on robust halo mass estimation for large galaxy group catalogs, with an emphasis on reducing systematic bias and improving precision.

Focus

  • Feature design from group observables.
  • Model comparison across tree-based and neural methods.
  • Validation against simulations and observed scaling relations.

Outcome

The framework supports more reliable halo-based studies of quenching, environment, and mass assembly history.
